items.title: How Open Models Are Driving AI Research
items.description: Every year, the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) reveals where thousands of AI researchers have decided to put their work.  This year’s accepted papers reveal a clear direction: open frontier models and open AI infrastructure have become foundational to how modern AI science gets done. NVIDIA had 74 papers accepted at ICML 2026. Approximately […]
items.content: Every year, the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) reveals where thousands of AI researchers have decided to put their work. This year’s accepted papers reveal a clear direction: open frontier models and open AI infrastructure have become foundational to how modern AI science gets done. NVIDIA had 74 papers accepted at ICML 2026. Approximately 2,000 accepted papers cite NVIDIA GPUs, and 145 cite NVIDIA Nemotron — a family of open models, including open datasets — as the foundation for new research. Hundreds more draw on NVIDIA Cosmos, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T, BioNeMo and other NVIDIA open model families, spanning physical AI, robotics, autonomous vehicles and biomedical research. The Themes Defining This Year’s Research Areas including vision and video generation, reinforcement learning for large language models (LLMs) and agent training as well as AI inference remained prominent themes across this year’s papers, reflecting sustained investment these fields command — while several new areas also broke through. Robot world models drew significant attention, with papers like DreamDojo pushing the boundary of how AI systems learn to reason about and act in physical environments. DreamDojo, for example, learns how the physical world behaves from human video and builds on NVIDIA Cosmos open frontier models to predict how a robot would handle objects and operate in environments it was never trained on. It lets researchers evaluate policies, plan actions and teleoperate a virtual robot, accelerating development without the costs and risks of physical deployment. AI for life sciences was fueled by NVIDIA BioNeMo open models and research contributions that help researchers understand protein function, molecular behavior and genetic code. Papers like FLIP2 introduce public benchmarks for testing how well AI predicts the effects of protein mutations. KERMT is a new BioNeMo open model for predicting molecular properties important to drug discovery. Synthetic data generation (SDG) drew particular interest at ICML this year with several Nemotron and physical AI open datasets, reflecting a broader shift in how researchers are thinking about training at scale without relying solely on human-labeled data. The Open Research Stack Open infrastructure gives researchers the tools to accelerate breakthroughs. The papers show Nemotron being used less like a single model release and more like a research stack: open weights to evaluate against, open datasets to train and adapt with, and open recipes for reasoning, tool use, safety, data curation and efficient inference. Alongside the models, NeMo Curator and the open datasets it supports gives researchers a reproducible foundation for training data curation. SDG tools enable creating high-quality training sets at a scale and speed that would’ve been impractical just a few years ago. The Cosmos 3 family of open, frontier omnimodels gives researchers and developers a generational leap in the ability to build robots, autonomous vehicles and vision AI that perceive, reason, plan and act in the physical world. In addition, the NVIDIA Alpamayo open model family for autonomous vehicle development, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T for robotics and NVIDIA BioNeMo for biomedical help accelerate research and development across industries. The Ecosystem Building on Top The momentum extends beyond NVIDIA’s own research labs. Basecamp Research developed a new DNA foundation model, EDEN, that helps researchers interpret and design genetic sequences. Merck & Co., uses KERMT to predict how potential drug molecules may behave in the body, including whether they are likely to be effective, safe and developable. Sakana AI — attending ICML this year — built its Fugu and Fugu-Ultra models directly on Nemotron 3 Ultra, using the open foundation to push forward its work on AI research automation. KiloCode integrated Nemotron into its code-routing architecture, reporting token cost reductions of up to 90% — a result with real implications for the economics of deploying AI in production. NAVER developed its own model using the Nemotron architecture, extending the foundation for Korean-language AI research. Together AI is hosting Nemotron models on its platform, making them more accessible to researchers who need reliable, seamless access to open inference. Humanoid, LG Electronics, NEURA Robotics and Noble Machines are adopting NVIDIA Isaac GR00T models to accelerate industrial deployments of their humanoids, while 1X, Agility, Agile Robots, Boston Dynamics, Hexagon Robotics, and Mentee are building the next generation of humanoids using Cosmos world models, Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab to accelerate the development and validation of their robots. Explore NVIDIA’s open models on Hugging Face. Explore genomics and biology research at ICML’s GenBio Workshop on Friday, July 10.
items.author: JJ Kim
items.titleZh: 开放模型如何推动AI研究
items.descriptionZh:
每年国际机器学习大会（ICML）都是全球AI研究者成果发布的晴雨表。今年ICML 2026的接受论文揭示了一个清晰的方向：开放前沿模型与开放AI基础设施已成为现代AI科学研究的基石。NVIDIA共有74篇论文被大会接收，约2000篇接受论文引用NVIDIA GPU，145篇引用NVIDIA Nemotron——一个包含开放数据集在内的开放模型家族。另有数百篇论文借助NVIDIA Cosmos、NVIDIA Isaac GR00T、BioNeMo等开放模型家族开展研究，覆盖物理AI、机器人、自动驾驶和生物医学等领域。

**今年研究主题的演变**  
视觉与视频生成、大型语言模型（LLM）的强化学习、智能体训练以及AI推理依然是本年度论文的突出主题，反映出这些领域持续获得大量投入。与此同时，多个新兴方向取得突破。机器人世界模型受到广泛关注，例如DreamDojo这篇工作，它推动AI系统在物理环境中学习和推理的能力边界。DreamDojo通过人类视频学习物理世界的行为规律，并基于NVIDIA Cosmos开放前沿模型，预测机器人如何处理从未训练过的物体和在陌生环境中操作。它允许研究人员评估策略、规划动作、远程操作虚拟机器人，从而在避免物理部署成本和风险的前提下加速开发。

AI生命科学领域则因NVIDIA BioNeMo开放模型和研究贡献而蓬勃发展，帮助研究者理解蛋白质功能、分子行为和遗传密码。例如，FLIP2引入了用于测试AI预测蛋白质突变效果能力的公开基准；KERMT是一个新的BioNeMo开放模型，用于预测与药物发现相关的分子特性。合成数据生成（SDG）在今年的ICML上尤其引人注目，多篇研究借助Nemotron和物理AI开放数据集，反映出研究人员不再单纯依赖人工标注数据，而是转向大规模训练的新范式。

**开放研究栈的架构**  
开放基础设施为研究者提供了加速突破的工具。论文显示，Nemotron已不再被视为单一模型发布，而更像一个研究栈：包含可供评比的开放权重、可训练和适配的开放数据集，以及用于推理、工具使用、安全性、数据整理和高效率推理的开放配方。配合Nemotron，NeMo Curator及其支持的开放数据集为训练数据整理提供了可复现的基础。SDG工具能够以几年前还不切实际的规模和速度创建高质量训练集。Cosmos 3系列开放前沿全能模型（Omnimodels）为研究人员和开发者提供了代际跃升的能力，用于构建能够感知、推理、规划并在物理世界中行动的机器人、自动驾驶汽车和视觉AI。此外，用于自动驾驶开发的NVIDIA Alpamayo开放模型家族、用于机器人的NVIDIA Isaac GR00T、用于生物医学的NVIDIA BioNeMo，正在加速跨行业的研发进程。

**生态系统的扩展与落地**  
这一势头远超NVIDIA自身的研究实验室。Basecamp Research开发了新的DNA基础模型EDEN，帮助研究者解读和设计基因序列。默克（Merck & Co.）利用KERMT模型预测潜在药物分子在体内的行为，包括其有效性、安全性和可开发性。Sakana AI（参加本届ICML）基于Nemotron 3 Ultra直接构建了Fugu和Fugu-Ultra模型，借助开放基础推进其AI研究自动化工作。KiloCode将Nemotron集成到其代码路由架构中，报告称token成本降低高达90%——这一成果对AI在生产环境中的部署经济学具有实际意义。NAVER利用Nemotron架构开发了针对韩语AI研究的扩展模型。Together AI在其平台上托管Nemotron模型，使需要可靠、无缝开放推理访问的研究者更容易获得。在人形机器人领域，Humanoid、LG电子、NEURA Robotics和Noble Machines正在采用NVIDIA Isaac GR00T模型加速其工业部署；而1X、Agility、Agile Robots、波士顿动力、Hexagon Robotics和Mentee等公司则利用Cosmos世界模型、Isaac Sim和Isaac Lab构建下一代人形机器人，加快其开发与验证。

**探索与参与**  
读者可在Hugging Face上探索NVIDIA的开放模型，并关注7月10日（周五）ICML的GenBio研讨会，了解基因组学与生物学研究的最新进展。

items.title: AMD Ryzen AI Halo review: AMD builds a DGX Spark of its own
items.description: The Ryzen AI Halo is a turn-key AMD local AI box that’s backed up with first-party software support, handy utilities, and plenty of documentation for local AI explorers. But the performance and application compatibility of the Ryzen AI Max+ 395 (aka Strix Halo) SoC still trails Nvidia’s GB10, and it’s pricey for what it offers.
items.content: Nvidia's DGX Spark and its GB10 SoC have set the template for what a purpose-built local AI developer sandbox should be. The combination of a standardized hardware platform with robust first-party software support and thorough documentation lets those curious about local AI get up and running faster than buying a bare-metal box and building everything up from scratch, especially in the rapidly evolving AI space.AMD's Ryzen AI Max+ 395, aka Strix Halo, SoC, is the best x86 spoiler for GB10 so far. It has the same 128GB of unified memory, a powerful 16C/32T Zen 5 CPU, and a Radeon 8060S integrated GPU with 2560 RDNA 3.5 stream processors. It also has an AMD XDNA 2 NPU for those who want to experiment with that accelerator in addition to the general-purpose Radeon GPU. And it can run Windows and Windows apps natively, whereas GB10 boxes are Linux-only for now.AMD's partners have been building around this hardware for about a year and a half, and it's a well-known quantity at this point. But once you have that hardware in hand, setting it up for AI workloads involves digging through scattered GitHub pages, Reddit threads, and AMD official documentation to get all the software pieces lined up right for the best performance and compatibility.AMD is trying to change all that today with the launch of the Ryzen AI Halo, a first-party, turn-key Strix Halo mini-PC that puts local AI first. This system can be had with Windows or Linux, and at least in the Linux form we're testing today, it comes preloaded with the full AMD ROCm software stack and an assortment of applications you need to immediately start generating tokens with your preferred model.And on the support side, AMD has taken a page directly out of Nvidia’s book and cooked up an entire set of its own playbooks that cover various local AI applications and usage scenarios with the AI Halo (and Strix Halo systems more generally) to serve as a springboard for local AI explorers.The grand tour (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The AI Halo comes wrapped in a plastic shell with a subtly color-shifting finish. It's got a large light bar ringing its front and sides that indicates system status. White means it's awake, while a pulsing blue indicates that it's asleep, assuming you allow it to suspend at all. Red indicates a fault. If you find the LED strip distracting, you can just turn it off using the preinstalled AI Developer Center app.The AI Halo has air intakes on its top and sides, and AMD cautions that you shouldn't block any of these intakes. If you're running by the book, that means this system is less flexible than it could be for space-constrained or multi-node home lab setups, where turning the unit on its side would allow for valuable space savings.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsEnterprising community members will likely design and share 3D-printed spacers and risers to get around these limitations, but for a device that is presumably meant to be used in home labs and production environments, the lack of flexibility in orientation is a small but annoying oversight. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Around back, the AI Halo has the same trio of USB Type-C ports you'll find on Nvidia GB10 boxes, plus one more for power input with the included 240W brick. The port closest to the power plug runs at “USB 3.2” speeds, while ports 3 and 4 are higher-speed USB 4. These ports are all DisplayPort Alt Mode compatible, or you can use the HDMI 2.1 port for display output if you prefer.For wired networking, the AI Halo offers a 10 Gigabit Ethernet port. That’s certainly fast, and AMD has written a clustering playbook for multiple AI Halos using that interface, but it’s in a whole other league compared to the 200Gbps ConnectX-7 NIC on the DGX Spark and its ilk. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)We didn't want to strip our AI Halo all the way down to its guts, but each of the four rubber feet on the bottom of the system is secured with a pair of tiny magnets, and they conceal the four screws you presumably need to remove to get further inside.Here’s a quick look at this system’s specs:Swipe to scroll horizontallyRyzen AI HaloCPUAMD Ryzen™ AI Max+ 395 Processor — 16 cores, 32 threads, “Zen 5” architectureGPUAMD Radeon™ 8060S Integrated GraphicsNPU AMD XDNA™ 2 NPUSoC TDP120WMemory128GB LPDDR5X, 8000 MT/s, 256GB memory bandwidthStorage2TB NVMe SSD USB3x USB-C ports (one USB 3.2 Gen 2, two USB 4), 1x USB-C for power inputNetworking1x 10 Gigabit Ethernet Wi-Fi 7 Bluetooth 5.4Display outputs USB-C DisplayPort Alt ModeHDMI 2.1Operating systemLinux (customized Debian) or Windows 11Dimensions150 x 150 x 45.4 mm (5.9 x 5.9 x 1.79 in)Amid the ongoing RAMpocalypse and NANDpocalyse, no Ryzen AI Max+ 395 system with 128GB of RAM and a large SSD is cheap, assuming you can find a 128GB config in stock anywhere.Even against that backdrop, the $3999 price tag for the AI Halo that we’re testing today is a pricey proposition. That sticker puts it at the low end of Nvidia GB10 systems like the Asus Ascent GX10 (albeit in its 1TB config).Our past testing of Strix Halo versus GB10 for local AI workloads has decisively put Nvidia’s platform on top, so this is a potentially awkward place for the AI Halo to land. Let’s dig in and find out if anything has changed.MORE: Best Graphics CardsMORE: GPU Benchmarks and HierarchyMORE: All Graphics Content
items.author: Jeffrey Kampman
items.titleZh: AMD Ryzen AI Halo 评测：AMD 打造自家版 DGX Spark
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达的DGX Spark及其GB10 SoC为本地AI开发者沙箱树立了标杆：标准化的硬件平台、强大的第一方软件支持和详尽文档，让本地AI爱好者能快速上手，远快于从零搭建裸机系统——特别是在迭代迅速的AI领域。AMD的Ryzen AI Max+ 395（代号Strix Halo）SoC是迄今为止对GB10最有力的x86挑战者，同样配备128GB统一内存、16核32线程Zen 5 CPU、集成2560个RDNA 3.5流处理器的Radeon 8060S GPU，以及用于实验NPU加速的AMD XDNA 2 NPU。与目前仅支持Linux的GB10不同，Strix Halo可原生运行Windows及Windows应用。

然而，过去一年半里，AMD合作伙伴围绕该硬件构建的系统虽已成熟，但用户拿到硬件后仍需翻遍分散的GitHub页面、Reddit帖子与AMD官方文档来拼凑软件堆栈，才能获得最佳性能与兼容性。AMD今天发布的Ryzen AI Halo正试图改变这一局面：这是一款将本地AI作为首要任务的Strix Halo一体化迷你PC，可预装Windows或Linux（本次评测为Linux版），出厂即预装完整AMD ROCm软件栈及多种应用，用户可立即开始用喜欢的模型生成token。在支持方面，AMD直接借鉴英伟达的策略，为AI Halo（及更广泛的Strix Halo系统）编写了覆盖多种本地AI应用场景的完整操作手册，作为探索者的跳板。

外观上，AI Halo采用带渐变效果的塑料外壳，前置与侧边环绕LED灯条指示状态：白色代表唤醒，蓝色脉动代表休眠（若允许挂起），红色代表故障。可通过预装的AI Developer Center应用关闭灯条。顶部与侧面设有进气口，AMD警告勿堵塞，这在空间受限或多节点家庭实验室中限制了摆放灵活性（无法侧放节省空间），预期社区会设计3D打印垫片来弥补，但对这款面向家庭实验室和产线的设备而言，方向限制仍是令人烦恼的小疏漏。

背面提供三个USB-C接口（与GB10设备相同），另加一个专用电源输入USB-C（配240W电源适配器）。靠近电源的接口为USB 3.2 Gen2，另外两个为USB 4且兼容DisplayPort Alt Mode，也可通过HDMI 2.1输出显示。有线网络提供10GbE端口，AMD已为此接口编写多节点集群操作手册，但相较于DGX Spark等采用的200Gbps ConnectX-7网卡，10GbE的带宽差距悬殊。

底部四个橡胶脚垫由磁铁固定，下方隐藏四颗螺丝用于拆解。快速规格如下：CPU为AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395（16核32线程，Zen 5架构），GPU为Radeon 8060S集成显卡，NPU为AMD XDNA 2，SoC TDP 120W，内存128GB LPDDR5X（8000 MT/s，256GB带宽），存储2TB NVMe SSD，三个USB-C（一个USB 3.2 Gen2，两个USB 4），一个电源输入USB-C，网络包括10GbE、Wi-Fi 7、蓝牙5.4，显示输出支持USB-C DisplayPort Alt Mode和HDMI 2.1，操作系统可选Linux（定制Debian）或Windows 11，尺寸150×150×45.4mm。

在当前内存和NAND涨价潮中，128GB RAM加大容量SSD的Ryzen AI Max+ 395系统价格不菲——即使能找到现货。本次评测的AI Halo定价3999美元，处于英伟达GB10系统（如华硕Ascent GX10的1TB版）的低价位区间。但我们此前的测试显示，Strix Halo在本地AI工作负载上的表现明显不及GB10，因此这个价位对AI Halo而言略显尴尬——让我们深入评测，看看是否有任何改观。

items.title: Nvidia and Intel tout homegrown American chip supply chain prowess as country bolsters local production, but gaps remain — crucial Blackwell packaging steps remain offshore as projects grow in scope and scale
items.description: America's AI supply chain now starts and ends in the U.S., while its most valuable middle steps remain entirely offshore until at least 2028.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia shouted proudly in a recent blog post that its network of American manufacturing partners and suppliers now spans 43 states, that TSMC's Phoenix plant is producing Blackwell wafers at volume, and that it plans to produce up to $500 billion of AI infrastructure in the U.S. over four years with partners including TSMC, Foxconn, Wistron, Corning, Coherent, and Amkor. Intel has made its own case in an America 250 post presenting end-to-end U.S. capabilities across design, manufacturing, and advanced packaging.Both accounts hold up at the wafer stage but omit the same downstream step: every Blackwell die that leaves TSMC's Arizona fab still crosses the Pacific to be packaged, no HBM is manufactured or packaged on U.S. soil, and the facilities intended to close those gaps won’t start production until 2028 at the earliest.Lofty projectsFoxconn is building a Houston factory to produce GB300 tray modules for Nvidia, and Wistron will assemble and test Nvidia AI systems at a new facility in Fort Worth, Texas. Coherent broke ground in June on an expanded Sherman, Texas, plant that the company describes as the first volume-production 6-inch indium phosphide fab, supplying the lasers and optical components that link AI systems together.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Corning is adding more than 3,000 jobs across optical manufacturing sites in North Carolina and Texas. The post also cites an estimate from consultancy Public First that Nvidia-driven AI demand will contribute $485 billion to U.S. GDP in 2026 and support over 100,000 jobs. “AI is driving a once-in-a-generation opportunity to reinvigorate American manufacturing and supply chains,” said Nvidia’s Jensen Huang in the post.Meanwhile, Intel's post lists R&D and manufacturing across Oregon, Arizona, New Mexico, and California, describes Ohio as “a planned site,” and devotes most of its length to workforce programs, K-12 AI education, and the company’s America250 partnership. Neither post addresses where the most advanced AI processors are actually assembled into finished chips. You may like Nvidia's exposure to Asian supply chains for components hits 90% of its production costs Google reportedly books Intel for packaging more than 3 million TPUs in 2028 Intel's fab roadmap examined — Arizona, Ohio, Ireland, and the two deadlines deciding 14A process node The Pacific round-trip Nvidia and TSMC produced the first Blackwell wafer at Fab 21 near Phoenix last October, and the site has since moved to volume output of Blackwell silicon on TSMC's 4NP node, the custom 4nm-class process built for Nvidia. On the other side of the Phoenix metro area, Intel's Fab 52 became fully operational in the same month as the first high-volume home of Intel 18A, and Naga Chandrasekaran, Intel's chief technology and operations officer, told CNBC in December that the fab is capable of more than 10,000 18A wafer starts per week. Panther Lake reached broad availability in January, Clearwater Forest is due in the first half of this year, and 18A yields are expected to reach industry-standard levels in early 2027, which I covered in my examination of Intel's fab roadmap.This ultimately means that leading-edge logic wafers are now being fabbed in the U.S. by two companies on two competing nodes. That’s a genuine change and, by any measure, a monolithic achievement when compared to the start of the decade, and neither company overstates that in their corporate blogs.However, a Blackwell data center GPU pairs two reticle-sized compute dies with eight stacks of HBM3e on a silicon interposer using TSMC's CoWoS-L packaging, and all of TSMC's CoWoS capacity is located in Taiwan. TSMC’s U.S. facilities currently send 100% of their chips to Taiwan for packaging, including wafers fabbed in Phoenix. A Blackwell die fabbed in Arizona therefore travels roughly 7,000 miles to be diced, stacked, and mounted, then travels onward through system assembly before any of it returns to a U.S. data center.As for HBM, every stack in production today comes out of SK hynix and Samsung facilities in South Korea or Micron's fabs in Taiwan and Japan, and the ABF substrates beneath the interposer are similarly concentrated in Japan and Taiwan. No U.S. facility currently manufactures or packages HBM. The one company running advanced packaging at scale on U.S. soil is Intel, whose Foveros operation in New Mexico handles its own 3D-stacked products and has started attracting outside interest; Google has reportedly booked Intel to package more than 3 million TPUs in 2028. Intel doesn’t currently appear anywhere in Nvidia's list of manufacturing partners.Nothing before 2029Amkor also broke ground on its Peoria, Arizona campus last October, a $7 billion, two-phase project with up to 750,000 square feet of cleanroom, roughly $400 million in CHIPS Act funding, and Apple and Nvidia signed as lead customers. Its first factory will be completed in mid-2027, with production beginning in early 2028. TSMC formalized the relationship on June 16th, signing a 10-year agreement under which it will procure packaging and test services from Amkor, while TSMC executives said in April that the foundry's own Arizona packaging facility will bring CoWoS and 3D-IC capacity online before 2029.SK hynix began initial work in April on its $3.87 billion advanced packaging plant in West Lafayette, Indiana, targeting mass production of HBM4E and HBM5 in the second half of 2028, the same window Amkor’s aiming for. The timing means the entire Blackwell family, and likely the first Rubin generation, will complete their product lifecycles without a fully domestic manufacturing path. The first AI accelerators that can be fabbed, packaged, and fitted with U.S.-packaged memory without leaving the country will be HBM4E-era parts arriving around 2028 to 2029.Unfortunately, the Section 48D advanced manufacturing tax credit, raised to 35% last July, doesn’t apply to projects whose construction begins after December 31st, 2026, which gives Coherent's June groundbreaking, SK hynix's April piling work, and Amkor's October start a shared fiscal deadline if they want to benefit from it.As for Foxconn and Wistron’s Houston and Fort Worth plants, they’ll receive GPUs packaged in Taiwan and assemble them into trays, racks, and systems on U.S. soil. It’s that type of assembly work that’s carrying most of the $500 billion figure, which counts the value of AI infrastructure produced rather than capital spent on factories. Wafers are American, racks are American, but everything in between isn’t. Whether that changes on schedule is a question for 2028, and it depends highly on two packaging campuses in Arizona and one in Indiana meeting their deadlines.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 英伟达和英特尔宣扬美国本土芯片供应链实力，但差距犹存——关键Blackwell封装步骤仍在海外，项目规模不断扩大
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达近日在官方博客中高调宣称，其美国制造合作伙伴和供应商网络已扩展至43个州，台积电位于凤凰城的工厂正在量产Blackwell晶圆，并计划在未来四年内与台积电、富士康、纬创、康宁、Coherent和Amkor等合作伙伴，在美国生产价值高达5000亿美元的AI基础设施。与此同时，英特尔在其“America 250”宣传稿中强调，自身在美国已具备从设计、制造到先进封装的全链条能力。

这两份宣传在晶圆制造环节都符合事实，但都刻意回避了同一个下游环节：每一片从台积电亚利桑那工厂出厂的Blackwell晶圆都必须横跨太平洋进行封装；目前没有任何高带宽存储器（HBM）在美国本土制造或封装；而计划填补这些空白的工厂，最早要到2028年才能投产。

### 宏大项目背后的现实

富士康正在休斯顿建设一座工厂，用于生产英伟达的GB300托盘模块；纬创将在德克萨斯州沃斯堡的新工厂组装和测试英伟达AI系统。Coherent于6月在德克萨斯州谢尔曼破土动工扩建工厂，该公司称这是首座6英寸磷化铟量产晶圆厂，将为连接AI系统提供激光器和光学组件。康宁则在北卡罗来纳州和德克萨斯州的光学制造基地新增超过3000个工作岗位。英伟达的博文还援引咨询公司Public First的估算，称由英伟达驱动的AI需求将在2026年为美国GDP贡献4850亿美元，并支持超过10万个就业岗位。英伟达CEO黄仁勋在文中宣称：“AI正推动一个千载难逢的机遇，重振美国制造业和供应链。”

与此同时，英特尔在博文中列出了在俄勒冈州、亚利桑那州、新墨西哥州和加利福尼亚州的研发和制造布局，将俄亥俄州描述为“规划中的场地”，并用大量篇幅介绍劳动力培训项目、K-12 AI教育以及与美国250周年纪念的合作。两份博文都没有提及最先进的AI处理器实际上是**在哪里组装成成品芯片的**。

### 跨越太平洋的往返旅程

英伟达和台积电去年10月在凤凰城附近的Fab 21工厂生产了首片Blackwell晶圆，此后该工厂已转而采用台积电专为英伟达定制的4纳米级工艺（4NP节点）量产Blackwell芯片。在凤凰城大都市区的另一边，英特尔的Fab 52工厂已于同月全面投产，成为英特尔18A工艺的首个量产基地。英特尔首席技术和运营官Naga Chandrasekaran去年12月对CNBC表示，该工厂每周可生产超过1万片18A晶圆。Panther Lake芯片已于1月实现广泛供货，Clearwater Forest定于今年上半年面世，18A工艺的良率预计在2027年初达到行业标准水平。

这意味着，先进逻辑晶圆现在确实由两家公司在美国本土、用两种竞争性工艺节点制造。与2020年代初相比，这无疑是一个巨大的成就，两家公司在自己的企业博客中都没有夸大这一点。

然而，一块Blackwell数据中心GPU的构成是：两片光罩尺寸的计算芯片与八堆HBM3e内存，通过硅中介层采用台积电的CoWoS-L封装技术整合在一起。而台积电所有的CoWoS产能都位于台湾。台积电在美国的工厂目前将100%的芯片（包括凤凰城生产的晶圆）送往台湾进行封装。因此，一块在亚利桑那制造的Blackwell晶圆需要飞行大约7000英里，才能被切割、堆叠和安装，然后继续运往系统组装地点，最后才能进入美国的数据中心。

至于HBM，目前生产中的每一堆都来自韩国SK海力士和三星的工厂，或者美光在中国台湾和日本的工厂；中介层下方的ABF基板同样高度集中在日本和中国台湾。美国目前没有任何工厂能够制造或封装HBM。目前唯一在美国本土大规模运行先进封装的是英特尔，其新墨西哥州的Foveros封装厂负责处理自家的3D堆叠产品，并已开始吸引外部客户——据报道，谷歌已预订英特尔2028年封装超过300万颗TPU的产能。然而，英特尔目前并不在英伟达公布的制造合作伙伴名单中。

### 最早2029年前无法实现

Amkor去年10月也在亚利桑那州皮奥里亚破土动工，建设一个价值70亿美元、分两期推进的项目，包括多达75万平方英尺的洁净室，获得约4亿美元的《芯片法案》资助，苹果和英伟达已签约成为主要客户。其第一座工厂将于2027年中期完工，2028年初开始生产。台积电于6月16日正式与Amkor签署了为期十年的协议，将向其采购封装和测试服务。而台积电高管在4月表示，台积电自己在亚利桑那的封装厂将在2029年之前上线CoWoS和3D IC产能。

SK海力士于4月在印第安纳州西拉斐特启动了38.7亿美元的先进封装工厂的初期工作，目标是在2028年下半年量产HBM4E和HBM5，这与Amkor的目标时间窗口一致。这意味着，整个Blackwell系列，甚至很可能第一代Rubin系列，都将在其完整的产品生命周期内无法实现完全本土化的制造路径。第一批能够在晶圆制造、封装和配备美国本土封装内存后无需离开美国本土的AI加速器，将是HBM4E时代的芯片，预计在2028年至2029年左右问世。

具有讽刺意味的是，去年7月提高至35%的Section 48D先进制造税收抵免，不适用于2026年12月31日之后开始建设的项目。这意味着Coherent的6月破土、SK海力士的4月打桩、以及Amkor的10月开工，如果想要享受这一税收优惠，都面临着共同的财务截止日期。

至于富士康和纬创分别在休斯顿和沃斯堡的工厂，它们将接收在台湾封装好的GPU，然后在美国本土将它们组装成托盘、机架和系统。正是这种组装工作，支撑了5000亿美元这个数字的绝大部分——该数字计算的是“生产的AI基础设施价值”，而非工厂的资本支出。晶圆是美国的，机架是美国的，但两者之间的一切都不是。这一局面能否按计划改变，取决于2028年，并高度依赖于亚利桑那州的两个封装园区和印第安纳州的一个封装园区能否按时完工。

items.title: China’s Huawei to enter South Korean AI chip market with new Atlas SuperPods, clusters pack 8,192 Ascend 950 accelerators per deployment — reportedly challenges Nvidia dominance with 'tripled inference performance' of H20 at one-quarter the cost
items.description: Huawei is reportedly preparing to enter South Korea's AI accelerator market with its Ascend 950 chips and Atlas 950 SuperPod, challenging Nvidia through aggressive pricing, amid a broader push to expand its AI ecosystem beyond China
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Huawei is planning to enter South Korea's AI accelerator market in the fourth quarter of 2026 with its Ascend 950 series processors and Atlas 950 SuperPod AI computing platform, according to a report by Korean publication ETNews last week. The move would mark Huawei's first major push into one of Nvidia's strongest overseas AI markets, with the Chinese company reportedly planning to undercut Nvidia on price while pitching its hardware as an alternative for customers seeking to reduce dependence on the US chipmaker.The chips spearheading the move are the Ascend 950PR and the Ascend 950DT, the latest models in Huawei's Ascend line of neural network processing units (NPUs) for AI computing. The Ascend 950PR, an inference-focused chip, entered mass production in April, while the Ascend 950DT, designed for AI training workloads, is scheduled for release in the fourth quarter. Both processors are expected to debut in Korea together with the Atlas 950 SuperPod, an integrated AI computing platform that Huawei says can scale to as many as 8,192 Ascend processors in a single deployment. (Image credit: Huawei)According to the report, Huawei Korea has completed master distributor agreements with two local partners, Hansol PNS and longtime Huawei collaborator SK Shieldus, and has already begun preparations for commercialization, including technical training, pricing policies, marketing strategies, and localized branding for the Korean market. Huawei is reportedly building its Korean campaign around aggressive pricing and processing power.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The company claims its Ascend 950PR delivers approximately 2.87 times the inference performance of Nvidia's H20 AI accelerator while costing around one-quarter as much. The H20 is Nvidia's export-compliant AI processor developed specifically for the Chinese market after US export restrictions prevented sales of more powerful GPUs. Huawei concedes its chip falls short on raw performance compared to Nvidia's flagship H200, but argues the gap can be closed by clustering thousands of Ascend processors together via the Atlas 950 platform.The Ascend 950 series uses Huawei's "self-developed" high-bandwidth memory (HBM), which the firm constructs from dies obtained from foreign sources. The 950PR uses Huawei's HiBL 1.0 memory and the 950DT its HiZQ 2.0 standard. You may like Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia’s H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo Huawei braces for $12 billion in AI chip revenue driven by homegrown AI model demand — Chinese fabs can barely keep up as Nvidia's market share craters within the region Huawei-led team claims it post-trained DeepSeek's 1.6-trillion-parameter model — 1,000 Ascend 910C chips used in training Huawei's strategy for the South Korea move, which comes as the country's demand for AI infrastructure surges, appears aimed at competing on both cost and ecosystem maturity, positioning its hardware as a viable Nvidia alternative. Nvidia's flagship accelerators reportedly command tens of thousands of dollars each, with supply remaining tight. A chip at one-quarter the price of the H20 gives Korean buyers a real incentive to seek a second source. Huawei also says it is improving compatibility between its Compute Architecture for Neural Networks (CANN) software stack and Nvidia's CUDA programming ecosystem to ease migration for developers.Huawei is not new to penetrating South Korea's market, having successfully entered the country’s highly competitive LTE equipment market in 2013. However, past experience is not a guarantee of future success. ETNews notes that industry observers expect Huawei to face resistance in Korea, citing local sensitivity toward Chinese technology, security concerns, the power and heat overhead of high-density Chinese silicon, and the vendor lock-in risk of adopting a proprietary stack. There is also a domestic dimension, as the Korean AI-chip scene is made up largely of accelerator startups, making Huawei's arrival — backed by its supply scale and software depth — read as a competitive threat.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 中国华为进军韩国AI芯片市场，推出全新Atlas SuperPods，每套部署集成8192个昇腾950加速器——据称以四分之一成本实现H20三倍推理性能，挑战英伟达主导地位
items.descriptionZh:
华为计划于2026年第四季度进军韩国AI加速器市场，将携其Ascend 950系列处理器和Atlas 950 SuperPod AI计算平台亮相。据韩国媒体ETNews上周报道，此举标志着华为首次在英伟达最强势的海外AI市场之一发起重大冲击。华为计划以低于英伟达的价格策略，同时向寻求减少对美国芯片制造商依赖的客户推销其硬件，作为替代方案。

此次行动的核心是Ascend 950PR和Ascend 950DT——华为Ascend系列神经网络处理单元（NPU）的最新型号，专为AI计算设计。其中，Ascend 950PR是一款面向推理的芯片，已于今年4月进入量产；而Ascend 950DT则针对AI训练工作负载，计划于2026年第四季度发布。两款处理器预计将与Atlas 950 SuperPod一同在韩国首次亮相。Atlas 950 SuperPod是华为推出的集成AI计算平台，据称单次部署可扩展至多达8192颗Ascend处理器。

根据报道，华为韩国已完成与两家本地合作伙伴的主分销协议，分别是Hansol PNS和长期合作方SK Shieldus，并已开始商业化准备，包括技术培训、定价政策、营销策略以及针对韩国市场的本地化品牌推广。华为正围绕激进定价和算力优势构建其韩国攻势。

华为声称，其Ascend 950PR的推理性能约为英伟达H20 AI加速器的2.87倍，而成本仅为后者的四分之一左右。H20是英伟达专门为中国市场开发的符合出口规则的AI处理器，此前美国出口限制阻止了更强大GPU的销售。华为承认其芯片在原始性能上不及英伟达旗舰级H200，但认为通过Atlas 950平台将数千颗Ascend处理器集群化，可以缩小差距。

Ascend 950系列采用华为“自研”的高带宽内存（HBM），该内存由从海外来源获得的裸片构建而成。其中950PR使用华为HiBL 1.0内存，950DT则采用HiZQ 2.0标准。

华为进入韩国市场的时机正值该国对AI基础设施需求激增之际。其策略似乎旨在同时在成本和生态成熟度上展开竞争，将其硬件定位为英伟达的可行替代方案。英伟达旗舰级加速器据称单价高达数万美元，且供应依然紧张。一颗价格仅为H20四分之一的芯片，为韩国买家提供了寻求第二供应商的真实动力。华为还表示，正在改善其计算架构神经网络（CANN）软件栈与英伟达CUDA编程生态系统的兼容性，以降低开发者的迁移难度。

华为对进入韩国市场并不陌生，曾于2013年成功打入该国竞争激烈的LTE设备市场。然而，过往经验并不能保证未来成功。ETNews指出，行业观察人士预计华为将在韩国面临阻力，理由包括当地对中国技术的敏感性、安全问题、高密度中国硅芯片带来的功耗和散热挑战，以及采用专有堆栈所导致的供应商锁定风险。此外，还有本土维度：韩国AI芯片领域主要由加速器初创公司构成，华为带着其供应规模和软件深度到来，被视为一种竞争威胁。

items.title: Wisconsin residents file class-action lawsuit against Microsoft's 'world's most powerful AI data center' due to data center noise — plaintiffs also mention construction noise and extreme light pollution from $7.3 billion facility
items.description: Wisconsin residents file class-action lawsuit against Microsoft due to data center noise — plaintiffs also mention construction noise and extreme light pollution
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Controversy due to AI data center buildouts generally centers around their massive power usage and draining of local water reserves, but noise is a third torment to nearby residents, and one that is arguably much harder to correct. Residents of Sturtevant, slightly south of Milwaukee, Wisconsin, filed a class-action lawsuit against Microsoft due to the excessive noise produced by the company's Fairwater facility. CEO Satya Nadella described the project as "the world's most powerful AI data center," projected to generate 865,000 tokens per second and have a final bill of $7.3 billion.The Sturtevant residents live just 1.5 miles (2.4 km) from the facility. The lawsuit was filed by three citizens and represents the households within this distance, reportedly amounting to over 1,000 homes, including areas in Mount Pleasant. The filing describes the noise situation as "not only excessive, but consistent and pervasive," and claims Microsoft did not "implement adequate acoustic barriers, shields, or walls that absorb, mitigate, and/or prevent the escape of noise, thereby resulting in the offsite emission of excessive noise beyond its property." One resident claims he had to change his shift at work to be able to sleep at all.Nearby residents also complain about excessive dust and traffic stemming from the construction work, as well as light pollution. One resident says he often can't see his house coming into town, while another claims that before the data center arrived, the sky was dark and full of stars, now mostly gone due to the bright lights.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: To some credit, Microsoft appears to have been trying to improve the situation, judging by a fairly detailed blog post on its community website. On June 18, the company said its engineers applied several measures that "fully resolved the issue," and that it would apply additional mitigations over the following months, including "additional sound reduction components."The backstory in the post says the firm was aware of the issue back in April, and "[it] did not expect the tonal quality of the sound to travel as far as it has," attributing the decibels to cooling fans operating at too-high speeds, now purportedly corrected. In an earlier project update about the data center, Microsoft says it would have street sweepers working 10 hours a day and limit construction to hours between 6am and 10pm. You may like AI data centers face increasing complaints about inaudible but 'felt' infrasound Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center The lawsuit was filed on July 1, indicating that either the issue isn't resolved, or that the particularly short distance between Fairwater 1 and Sturtevant might make for a conundrum that's exceedingly tricky to solve. Some Mount Pleasant residents even live across the street from the campus, too. Before the Fairwater data center arrived, the land was already zoned for heavy industrial use back in 2017 to Foxconn, a status that carried over to Microsoft upon purchase. Notably, Wisconsin's "direct legislation" apparently does not allow amending or repealing existing ordinances, and is only available to cities, not towns. items.titleZh: 威斯康星州居民因噪音问题对微软"世界最强大AI数据中心"提起集体诉讼——原告指责73亿美元设施造成建筑噪音和极端光污染
items.descriptionZh:
围绕AI数据中心建设引发的争议通常集中在巨大的电力消耗和当地水资源枯竭上，但噪音问题已成为困扰附近居民的第三大痛点，而且可以说是更难以纠正的难题。位于威斯康星州密尔沃基市以南的斯特蒂文特（Sturtevant）居民，因微软Fairwater数据中心产生的过度噪音，对其提起了集体诉讼。微软CEO萨提亚·纳德拉将该设施描述为“世界上最强大的AI数据中心”，预计每秒可生成86.5万个token（AI处理的基本单位），最终投资额高达73亿美元。

提起指控的斯特蒂文特居民距离该设施仅1.5英里（约2.4公里）。该诉讼由三名公民发起，代表这一距离范围内的家庭，据称涉及超过1000户住宅，包括快乐山（Mount Pleasant）的部分区域。诉状描述噪音状况“不仅过度，而且持续不断、无处不在”，并声称微软未能“实施足够的隔音屏障、防护墙或能够吸收、减轻和/或阻止噪音泄漏的墙体，从而导致过度噪音从其地产范围外泄”。一位居民声称，为了能睡个好觉，他不得不调整工作班次。

附近的居民还抱怨施工带来的过量粉尘和交通问题，以及光污染。一位居民说他经常无法在入城时看到自己的房子，而另一名居民则声称，在数据中心到来之前，天空是黑暗的，满天繁星，现在这一切大多因明亮的灯光而消失了。

值得肯定的是，从微软社区网站上相当详细的博客文章来看，该公司似乎一直在努力改善这一状况。6月18日，该公司表示其工程师采取了多项措施，“完全解决了问题”，并将在未来几个月内采取更多缓解措施，包括“额外的降噪组件”。

这篇后续文章的背景信息显示，该公司早在4月份就意识到了这个问题，并“[它]没有预料到声音的音调质量会传播得如此之远”，将分贝水平归因于以过高速度运行的冷却风扇，现已声称纠正。在一份关于该数据中心的早期项目更新报告中，微软表示每天将有街道清扫车工作10小时，并将施工时间限制在早上6点到晚上10点之间。

诉讼是在7月1日提起的，这表明要么问题尚未解决，要么Fairwater 1设施与斯特蒂文特之间特别短的距离可能构成了一个极其棘手的难题。有些快乐山的居民甚至就住在园区街对面。在Fairwater数据中心入驻之前，该地块早在2017年就已规划为面向富士康的重工业用途，这一规划状态在微软购买后得以延续。值得注意的是，威斯康星州的“直接立法”似乎不允许修改或废除现有的条例，并且仅对城市适用，而非城镇。

items.title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
items.description:
Most Americans don't trust AI. It's proven that it doesn't know what safe toppings for pizza are. People don't even want to listen to AI music. But none of that matters for some of America's wealthy, who are turning to AI to teach their kids instead of traditional schools.
Companies like Forge Prep and Alpha School are charging families tens of thousands of dollars to turn their kids into beta testers for AI tutors and "interactive project-based workshops." Unsurprisingly, Silicon Valley have been major adopters of this new model. Shaun Johnson, a San Francisco-based venture capitalist, told The Wall Street Journal that he plans to send his …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Most Americans don’t trust AI. It’s proven that it doesn’t know what safe toppings for pizza are. People don’t even want to listen to AI music. But none of that matters for some of America’s wealthy, who are turning to AI to teach their kids instead of traditional schools.Companies like Forge Prep and Alpha School are charging families tens of thousands of dollars to turn their kids into beta testers for AI tutors and “interactive project-based workshops.” Unsurprisingly, Silicon Valley have been major adopters of this new model. Shaun Johnson, a San Francisco-based venture capitalist, told The Wall Street Journal that he plans to send his son to a $75,000 year Alpha Kindergarten. He said, “We recognize that education is likely broken the way it is and there’s going to be entrepreneurs that try to fix it… You want someone to be able to think on their feet and navigate the world, not necessarily a recitation of facts in a particular discipline.”Ignoring Johnson’s fundamental lack of understanding about modern pedagogy, it’s unclear how notoriously sycophantic AI will train children to “think on their feet and navigate the world.” It’s also concerning that Alpha School cofounder MacKenzie Price has said she plans to keep “hot-button social issues” out of the classroom. Which, in the current political climate, could cover women’s rights, America’s history of slavery, and our immigrant past. That might not seem like a major issue when you’re talking about kindergarten, but in some locations, Alpha School goes through high school.Companies like Forge also don’t share performance metrics, so there’s no evidence that these AI-guided private schools are improving educational outcomes.
items.author: Terrence O’Brien
items.titleZh: 该国一些富人让AI教他们的孩子
items.descriptionZh:
大多数美国人对人工智能持不信任态度——有证据表明，AI连披萨上什么配料安全都不知道，人们甚至不愿听AI生成的音乐。但这些都不妨碍一部分美国富裕阶层将子女从传统学校转向AI教育。以Forge Prep和Alpha School为代表的公司向家庭收取数万美元费用，让孩子们成为AI导师及“互动式项目工作坊”的测试者。不出意料，硅谷成为这一新模式的主要拥趸。旧金山风险投资人肖恩·约翰逊（Shaun Johnson）向《华尔街日报》表示，他计划将儿子送入一年学费7.5万美元的Alpha幼儿园。他说：“我们认识到传统教育方式很可能已经失效，会有创业者试图修正它……我们希望孩子能灵活思考、应对世界，而不是死记硬背某个学科的事实。”

暂且不论约翰逊对现代教育学的根本性误解，关键在于——以谄媚迎合著称的AI如何训练孩子“灵活思考、应对世界”？更令人担忧的是，Alpha School联合创始人麦肯齐·普莱斯（MacKenzie Price）声称，她计划在课堂中避开“热点社会议题”。在当下的政治气候中，这可能会涵盖女性权利、美国奴隶制历史以及移民传统。对于幼儿园来说，这或许不是大问题，但在某些地区，Alpha School一直开设到高中。

此外，像Forge这样的公司并不公开绩效指标，因此没有任何证据表明这些AI引导的私立学校确实改善了教育成果。

history.title: How Open Models Are Driving AI Research
history.description: Every year, the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) reveals where thousands of AI researchers have decided to put their work.  This year’s accepted papers reveal a clear direction: open frontier models and open AI infrastructure have become foundational to how modern AI science gets done. NVIDIA had 74 papers accepted at ICML 2026. Approximately […]
history.content: Every year, the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) reveals where thousands of AI researchers have decided to put their work. This year’s accepted papers reveal a clear direction: open frontier models and open AI infrastructure have become foundational to how modern AI science gets done. NVIDIA had 74 papers accepted at ICML 2026. Approximately 2,000 accepted papers cite NVIDIA GPUs, and 145 cite NVIDIA Nemotron — a family of open models, including open datasets — as the foundation for new research. Hundreds more draw on NVIDIA Cosmos, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T, BioNeMo and other NVIDIA open model families, spanning physical AI, robotics, autonomous vehicles and biomedical research. The Themes Defining This Year’s Research Areas including vision and video generation, reinforcement learning for large language models (LLMs) and agent training as well as AI inference remained prominent themes across this year’s papers, reflecting sustained investment these fields command — while several new areas also broke through. Robot world models drew significant attention, with papers like DreamDojo pushing the boundary of how AI systems learn to reason about and act in physical environments. DreamDojo, for example, learns how the physical world behaves from human video and builds on NVIDIA Cosmos open frontier models to predict how a robot would handle objects and operate in environments it was never trained on. It lets researchers evaluate policies, plan actions and teleoperate a virtual robot, accelerating development without the costs and risks of physical deployment. AI for life sciences was fueled by NVIDIA BioNeMo open models and research contributions that help researchers understand protein function, molecular behavior and genetic code. Papers like FLIP2 introduce public benchmarks for testing how well AI predicts the effects of protein mutations. KERMT is a new BioNeMo open model for predicting molecular properties important to drug discovery. Synthetic data generation (SDG) drew particular interest at ICML this year with several Nemotron and physical AI open datasets, reflecting a broader shift in how researchers are thinking about training at scale without relying solely on human-labeled data. The Open Research Stack Open infrastructure gives researchers the tools to accelerate breakthroughs. The papers show Nemotron being used less like a single model release and more like a research stack: open weights to evaluate against, open datasets to train and adapt with, and open recipes for reasoning, tool use, safety, data curation and efficient inference. Alongside the models, NeMo Curator and the open datasets it supports gives researchers a reproducible foundation for training data curation. SDG tools enable creating high-quality training sets at a scale and speed that would’ve been impractical just a few years ago. The Cosmos 3 family of open, frontier omnimodels gives researchers and developers a generational leap in the ability to build robots, autonomous vehicles and vision AI that perceive, reason, plan and act in the physical world. In addition, the NVIDIA Alpamayo open model family for autonomous vehicle development, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T for robotics and NVIDIA BioNeMo for biomedical help accelerate research and development across industries. The Ecosystem Building on Top The momentum extends beyond NVIDIA’s own research labs. Basecamp Research developed a new DNA foundation model, EDEN, that helps researchers interpret and design genetic sequences. Merck & Co., uses KERMT to predict how potential drug molecules may behave in the body, including whether they are likely to be effective, safe and developable. Sakana AI — attending ICML this year — built its Fugu and Fugu-Ultra models directly on Nemotron 3 Ultra, using the open foundation to push forward its work on AI research automation. KiloCode integrated Nemotron into its code-routing architecture, reporting token cost reductions of up to 90% — a result with real implications for the economics of deploying AI in production. NAVER developed its own model using the Nemotron architecture, extending the foundation for Korean-language AI research. Together AI is hosting Nemotron models on its platform, making them more accessible to researchers who need reliable, seamless access to open inference. Humanoid, LG Electronics, NEURA Robotics and Noble Machines are adopting NVIDIA Isaac GR00T models to accelerate industrial deployments of their humanoids, while 1X, Agility, Agile Robots, Boston Dynamics, Hexagon Robotics, and Mentee are building the next generation of humanoids using Cosmos world models, Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab to accelerate the development and validation of their robots. Explore NVIDIA’s open models on Hugging Face. Explore genomics and biology research at ICML’s GenBio Workshop on Friday, July 10.
history.author: JJ Kim
history.titleZh: 开放模型如何推动AI研究
history.descriptionZh:
每年国际机器学习大会（ICML）都是全球AI研究者成果发布的晴雨表。今年ICML 2026的接受论文揭示了一个清晰的方向：开放前沿模型与开放AI基础设施已成为现代AI科学研究的基石。NVIDIA共有74篇论文被大会接收，约2000篇接受论文引用NVIDIA GPU，145篇引用NVIDIA Nemotron——一个包含开放数据集在内的开放模型家族。另有数百篇论文借助NVIDIA Cosmos、NVIDIA Isaac GR00T、BioNeMo等开放模型家族开展研究，覆盖物理AI、机器人、自动驾驶和生物医学等领域。

**今年研究主题的演变**  
视觉与视频生成、大型语言模型（LLM）的强化学习、智能体训练以及AI推理依然是本年度论文的突出主题，反映出这些领域持续获得大量投入。与此同时，多个新兴方向取得突破。机器人世界模型受到广泛关注，例如DreamDojo这篇工作，它推动AI系统在物理环境中学习和推理的能力边界。DreamDojo通过人类视频学习物理世界的行为规律，并基于NVIDIA Cosmos开放前沿模型，预测机器人如何处理从未训练过的物体和在陌生环境中操作。它允许研究人员评估策略、规划动作、远程操作虚拟机器人，从而在避免物理部署成本和风险的前提下加速开发。

AI生命科学领域则因NVIDIA BioNeMo开放模型和研究贡献而蓬勃发展，帮助研究者理解蛋白质功能、分子行为和遗传密码。例如，FLIP2引入了用于测试AI预测蛋白质突变效果能力的公开基准；KERMT是一个新的BioNeMo开放模型，用于预测与药物发现相关的分子特性。合成数据生成（SDG）在今年的ICML上尤其引人注目，多篇研究借助Nemotron和物理AI开放数据集，反映出研究人员不再单纯依赖人工标注数据，而是转向大规模训练的新范式。

**开放研究栈的架构**  
开放基础设施为研究者提供了加速突破的工具。论文显示，Nemotron已不再被视为单一模型发布，而更像一个研究栈：包含可供评比的开放权重、可训练和适配的开放数据集，以及用于推理、工具使用、安全性、数据整理和高效率推理的开放配方。配合Nemotron，NeMo Curator及其支持的开放数据集为训练数据整理提供了可复现的基础。SDG工具能够以几年前还不切实际的规模和速度创建高质量训练集。Cosmos 3系列开放前沿全能模型（Omnimodels）为研究人员和开发者提供了代际跃升的能力，用于构建能够感知、推理、规划并在物理世界中行动的机器人、自动驾驶汽车和视觉AI。此外，用于自动驾驶开发的NVIDIA Alpamayo开放模型家族、用于机器人的NVIDIA Isaac GR00T、用于生物医学的NVIDIA BioNeMo，正在加速跨行业的研发进程。

**生态系统的扩展与落地**  
这一势头远超NVIDIA自身的研究实验室。Basecamp Research开发了新的DNA基础模型EDEN，帮助研究者解读和设计基因序列。默克（Merck & Co.）利用KERMT模型预测潜在药物分子在体内的行为，包括其有效性、安全性和可开发性。Sakana AI（参加本届ICML）基于Nemotron 3 Ultra直接构建了Fugu和Fugu-Ultra模型，借助开放基础推进其AI研究自动化工作。KiloCode将Nemotron集成到其代码路由架构中，报告称token成本降低高达90%——这一成果对AI在生产环境中的部署经济学具有实际意义。NAVER利用Nemotron架构开发了针对韩语AI研究的扩展模型。Together AI在其平台上托管Nemotron模型，使需要可靠、无缝开放推理访问的研究者更容易获得。在人形机器人领域，Humanoid、LG电子、NEURA Robotics和Noble Machines正在采用NVIDIA Isaac GR00T模型加速其工业部署；而1X、Agility、Agile Robots、波士顿动力、Hexagon Robotics和Mentee等公司则利用Cosmos世界模型、Isaac Sim和Isaac Lab构建下一代人形机器人，加快其开发与验证。

**探索与参与**  
读者可在Hugging Face上探索NVIDIA的开放模型，并关注7月10日（周五）ICML的GenBio研讨会，了解基因组学与生物学研究的最新进展。

history.title: AMD Ryzen AI Halo review: AMD builds a DGX Spark of its own
history.description: The Ryzen AI Halo is a turn-key AMD local AI box that’s backed up with first-party software support, handy utilities, and plenty of documentation for local AI explorers. But the performance and application compatibility of the Ryzen AI Max+ 395 (aka Strix Halo) SoC still trails Nvidia’s GB10, and it’s pricey for what it offers.
history.content: Nvidia's DGX Spark and its GB10 SoC have set the template for what a purpose-built local AI developer sandbox should be. The combination of a standardized hardware platform with robust first-party software support and thorough documentation lets those curious about local AI get up and running faster than buying a bare-metal box and building everything up from scratch, especially in the rapidly evolving AI space.AMD's Ryzen AI Max+ 395, aka Strix Halo, SoC, is the best x86 spoiler for GB10 so far. It has the same 128GB of unified memory, a powerful 16C/32T Zen 5 CPU, and a Radeon 8060S integrated GPU with 2560 RDNA 3.5 stream processors. It also has an AMD XDNA 2 NPU for those who want to experiment with that accelerator in addition to the general-purpose Radeon GPU. And it can run Windows and Windows apps natively, whereas GB10 boxes are Linux-only for now.AMD's partners have been building around this hardware for about a year and a half, and it's a well-known quantity at this point. But once you have that hardware in hand, setting it up for AI workloads involves digging through scattered GitHub pages, Reddit threads, and AMD official documentation to get all the software pieces lined up right for the best performance and compatibility.AMD is trying to change all that today with the launch of the Ryzen AI Halo, a first-party, turn-key Strix Halo mini-PC that puts local AI first. This system can be had with Windows or Linux, and at least in the Linux form we're testing today, it comes preloaded with the full AMD ROCm software stack and an assortment of applications you need to immediately start generating tokens with your preferred model.And on the support side, AMD has taken a page directly out of Nvidia’s book and cooked up an entire set of its own playbooks that cover various local AI applications and usage scenarios with the AI Halo (and Strix Halo systems more generally) to serve as a springboard for local AI explorers.The grand tour (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The AI Halo comes wrapped in a plastic shell with a subtly color-shifting finish. It's got a large light bar ringing its front and sides that indicates system status. White means it's awake, while a pulsing blue indicates that it's asleep, assuming you allow it to suspend at all. Red indicates a fault. If you find the LED strip distracting, you can just turn it off using the preinstalled AI Developer Center app.The AI Halo has air intakes on its top and sides, and AMD cautions that you shouldn't block any of these intakes. If you're running by the book, that means this system is less flexible than it could be for space-constrained or multi-node home lab setups, where turning the unit on its side would allow for valuable space savings.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsEnterprising community members will likely design and share 3D-printed spacers and risers to get around these limitations, but for a device that is presumably meant to be used in home labs and production environments, the lack of flexibility in orientation is a small but annoying oversight. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Around back, the AI Halo has the same trio of USB Type-C ports you'll find on Nvidia GB10 boxes, plus one more for power input with the included 240W brick. The port closest to the power plug runs at “USB 3.2” speeds, while ports 3 and 4 are higher-speed USB 4. These ports are all DisplayPort Alt Mode compatible, or you can use the HDMI 2.1 port for display output if you prefer.For wired networking, the AI Halo offers a 10 Gigabit Ethernet port. That’s certainly fast, and AMD has written a clustering playbook for multiple AI Halos using that interface, but it’s in a whole other league compared to the 200Gbps ConnectX-7 NIC on the DGX Spark and its ilk. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)We didn't want to strip our AI Halo all the way down to its guts, but each of the four rubber feet on the bottom of the system is secured with a pair of tiny magnets, and they conceal the four screws you presumably need to remove to get further inside.Here’s a quick look at this system’s specs:Swipe to scroll horizontallyRyzen AI HaloCPUAMD Ryzen™ AI Max+ 395 Processor — 16 cores, 32 threads, “Zen 5” architectureGPUAMD Radeon™ 8060S Integrated GraphicsNPU AMD XDNA™ 2 NPUSoC TDP120WMemory128GB LPDDR5X, 8000 MT/s, 256GB memory bandwidthStorage2TB NVMe SSD USB3x USB-C ports (one USB 3.2 Gen 2, two USB 4), 1x USB-C for power inputNetworking1x 10 Gigabit Ethernet Wi-Fi 7 Bluetooth 5.4Display outputs USB-C DisplayPort Alt ModeHDMI 2.1Operating systemLinux (customized Debian) or Windows 11Dimensions150 x 150 x 45.4 mm (5.9 x 5.9 x 1.79 in)Amid the ongoing RAMpocalypse and NANDpocalyse, no Ryzen AI Max+ 395 system with 128GB of RAM and a large SSD is cheap, assuming you can find a 128GB config in stock anywhere.Even against that backdrop, the $3999 price tag for the AI Halo that we’re testing today is a pricey proposition. That sticker puts it at the low end of Nvidia GB10 systems like the Asus Ascent GX10 (albeit in its 1TB config).Our past testing of Strix Halo versus GB10 for local AI workloads has decisively put Nvidia’s platform on top, so this is a potentially awkward place for the AI Halo to land. Let’s dig in and find out if anything has changed.MORE: Best Graphics CardsMORE: GPU Benchmarks and HierarchyMORE: All Graphics Content
history.author: Jeffrey Kampman
history.titleZh: AMD Ryzen AI Halo 评测：AMD 打造自家版 DGX Spark
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达的DGX Spark及其GB10 SoC为本地AI开发者沙箱树立了标杆：标准化的硬件平台、强大的第一方软件支持和详尽文档，让本地AI爱好者能快速上手，远快于从零搭建裸机系统——特别是在迭代迅速的AI领域。AMD的Ryzen AI Max+ 395（代号Strix Halo）SoC是迄今为止对GB10最有力的x86挑战者，同样配备128GB统一内存、16核32线程Zen 5 CPU、集成2560个RDNA 3.5流处理器的Radeon 8060S GPU，以及用于实验NPU加速的AMD XDNA 2 NPU。与目前仅支持Linux的GB10不同，Strix Halo可原生运行Windows及Windows应用。

然而，过去一年半里，AMD合作伙伴围绕该硬件构建的系统虽已成熟，但用户拿到硬件后仍需翻遍分散的GitHub页面、Reddit帖子与AMD官方文档来拼凑软件堆栈，才能获得最佳性能与兼容性。AMD今天发布的Ryzen AI Halo正试图改变这一局面：这是一款将本地AI作为首要任务的Strix Halo一体化迷你PC，可预装Windows或Linux（本次评测为Linux版），出厂即预装完整AMD ROCm软件栈及多种应用，用户可立即开始用喜欢的模型生成token。在支持方面，AMD直接借鉴英伟达的策略，为AI Halo（及更广泛的Strix Halo系统）编写了覆盖多种本地AI应用场景的完整操作手册，作为探索者的跳板。

外观上，AI Halo采用带渐变效果的塑料外壳，前置与侧边环绕LED灯条指示状态：白色代表唤醒，蓝色脉动代表休眠（若允许挂起），红色代表故障。可通过预装的AI Developer Center应用关闭灯条。顶部与侧面设有进气口，AMD警告勿堵塞，这在空间受限或多节点家庭实验室中限制了摆放灵活性（无法侧放节省空间），预期社区会设计3D打印垫片来弥补，但对这款面向家庭实验室和产线的设备而言，方向限制仍是令人烦恼的小疏漏。

背面提供三个USB-C接口（与GB10设备相同），另加一个专用电源输入USB-C（配240W电源适配器）。靠近电源的接口为USB 3.2 Gen2，另外两个为USB 4且兼容DisplayPort Alt Mode，也可通过HDMI 2.1输出显示。有线网络提供10GbE端口，AMD已为此接口编写多节点集群操作手册，但相较于DGX Spark等采用的200Gbps ConnectX-7网卡，10GbE的带宽差距悬殊。

底部四个橡胶脚垫由磁铁固定，下方隐藏四颗螺丝用于拆解。快速规格如下：CPU为AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395（16核32线程，Zen 5架构），GPU为Radeon 8060S集成显卡，NPU为AMD XDNA 2，SoC TDP 120W，内存128GB LPDDR5X（8000 MT/s，256GB带宽），存储2TB NVMe SSD，三个USB-C（一个USB 3.2 Gen2，两个USB 4），一个电源输入USB-C，网络包括10GbE、Wi-Fi 7、蓝牙5.4，显示输出支持USB-C DisplayPort Alt Mode和HDMI 2.1，操作系统可选Linux（定制Debian）或Windows 11，尺寸150×150×45.4mm。

在当前内存和NAND涨价潮中，128GB RAM加大容量SSD的Ryzen AI Max+ 395系统价格不菲——即使能找到现货。本次评测的AI Halo定价3999美元，处于英伟达GB10系统（如华硕Ascent GX10的1TB版）的低价位区间。但我们此前的测试显示，Strix Halo在本地AI工作负载上的表现明显不及GB10，因此这个价位对AI Halo而言略显尴尬——让我们深入评测，看看是否有任何改观。

history.title: Nvidia and Intel tout homegrown American chip supply chain prowess as country bolsters local production, but gaps remain — crucial Blackwell packaging steps remain offshore as projects grow in scope and scale
history.description: America's AI supply chain now starts and ends in the U.S., while its most valuable middle steps remain entirely offshore until at least 2028.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia shouted proudly in a recent blog post that its network of American manufacturing partners and suppliers now spans 43 states, that TSMC's Phoenix plant is producing Blackwell wafers at volume, and that it plans to produce up to $500 billion of AI infrastructure in the U.S. over four years with partners including TSMC, Foxconn, Wistron, Corning, Coherent, and Amkor. Intel has made its own case in an America 250 post presenting end-to-end U.S. capabilities across design, manufacturing, and advanced packaging.Both accounts hold up at the wafer stage but omit the same downstream step: every Blackwell die that leaves TSMC's Arizona fab still crosses the Pacific to be packaged, no HBM is manufactured or packaged on U.S. soil, and the facilities intended to close those gaps won’t start production until 2028 at the earliest.Lofty projectsFoxconn is building a Houston factory to produce GB300 tray modules for Nvidia, and Wistron will assemble and test Nvidia AI systems at a new facility in Fort Worth, Texas. Coherent broke ground in June on an expanded Sherman, Texas, plant that the company describes as the first volume-production 6-inch indium phosphide fab, supplying the lasers and optical components that link AI systems together.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Corning is adding more than 3,000 jobs across optical manufacturing sites in North Carolina and Texas. The post also cites an estimate from consultancy Public First that Nvidia-driven AI demand will contribute $485 billion to U.S. GDP in 2026 and support over 100,000 jobs. “AI is driving a once-in-a-generation opportunity to reinvigorate American manufacturing and supply chains,” said Nvidia’s Jensen Huang in the post.Meanwhile, Intel's post lists R&D and manufacturing across Oregon, Arizona, New Mexico, and California, describes Ohio as “a planned site,” and devotes most of its length to workforce programs, K-12 AI education, and the company’s America250 partnership. Neither post addresses where the most advanced AI processors are actually assembled into finished chips. You may like Nvidia's exposure to Asian supply chains for components hits 90% of its production costs Google reportedly books Intel for packaging more than 3 million TPUs in 2028 Intel's fab roadmap examined — Arizona, Ohio, Ireland, and the two deadlines deciding 14A process node The Pacific round-trip Nvidia and TSMC produced the first Blackwell wafer at Fab 21 near Phoenix last October, and the site has since moved to volume output of Blackwell silicon on TSMC's 4NP node, the custom 4nm-class process built for Nvidia. On the other side of the Phoenix metro area, Intel's Fab 52 became fully operational in the same month as the first high-volume home of Intel 18A, and Naga Chandrasekaran, Intel's chief technology and operations officer, told CNBC in December that the fab is capable of more than 10,000 18A wafer starts per week. Panther Lake reached broad availability in January, Clearwater Forest is due in the first half of this year, and 18A yields are expected to reach industry-standard levels in early 2027, which I covered in my examination of Intel's fab roadmap.This ultimately means that leading-edge logic wafers are now being fabbed in the U.S. by two companies on two competing nodes. That’s a genuine change and, by any measure, a monolithic achievement when compared to the start of the decade, and neither company overstates that in their corporate blogs.However, a Blackwell data center GPU pairs two reticle-sized compute dies with eight stacks of HBM3e on a silicon interposer using TSMC's CoWoS-L packaging, and all of TSMC's CoWoS capacity is located in Taiwan. TSMC’s U.S. facilities currently send 100% of their chips to Taiwan for packaging, including wafers fabbed in Phoenix. A Blackwell die fabbed in Arizona therefore travels roughly 7,000 miles to be diced, stacked, and mounted, then travels onward through system assembly before any of it returns to a U.S. data center.As for HBM, every stack in production today comes out of SK hynix and Samsung facilities in South Korea or Micron's fabs in Taiwan and Japan, and the ABF substrates beneath the interposer are similarly concentrated in Japan and Taiwan. No U.S. facility currently manufactures or packages HBM. The one company running advanced packaging at scale on U.S. soil is Intel, whose Foveros operation in New Mexico handles its own 3D-stacked products and has started attracting outside interest; Google has reportedly booked Intel to package more than 3 million TPUs in 2028. Intel doesn’t currently appear anywhere in Nvidia's list of manufacturing partners.Nothing before 2029Amkor also broke ground on its Peoria, Arizona campus last October, a $7 billion, two-phase project with up to 750,000 square feet of cleanroom, roughly $400 million in CHIPS Act funding, and Apple and Nvidia signed as lead customers. Its first factory will be completed in mid-2027, with production beginning in early 2028. TSMC formalized the relationship on June 16th, signing a 10-year agreement under which it will procure packaging and test services from Amkor, while TSMC executives said in April that the foundry's own Arizona packaging facility will bring CoWoS and 3D-IC capacity online before 2029.SK hynix began initial work in April on its $3.87 billion advanced packaging plant in West Lafayette, Indiana, targeting mass production of HBM4E and HBM5 in the second half of 2028, the same window Amkor’s aiming for. The timing means the entire Blackwell family, and likely the first Rubin generation, will complete their product lifecycles without a fully domestic manufacturing path. The first AI accelerators that can be fabbed, packaged, and fitted with U.S.-packaged memory without leaving the country will be HBM4E-era parts arriving around 2028 to 2029.Unfortunately, the Section 48D advanced manufacturing tax credit, raised to 35% last July, doesn’t apply to projects whose construction begins after December 31st, 2026, which gives Coherent's June groundbreaking, SK hynix's April piling work, and Amkor's October start a shared fiscal deadline if they want to benefit from it.As for Foxconn and Wistron’s Houston and Fort Worth plants, they’ll receive GPUs packaged in Taiwan and assemble them into trays, racks, and systems on U.S. soil. It’s that type of assembly work that’s carrying most of the $500 billion figure, which counts the value of AI infrastructure produced rather than capital spent on factories. Wafers are American, racks are American, but everything in between isn’t. Whether that changes on schedule is a question for 2028, and it depends highly on two packaging campuses in Arizona and one in Indiana meeting their deadlines.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 英伟达和英特尔宣扬美国本土芯片供应链实力，但差距犹存——关键Blackwell封装步骤仍在海外，项目规模不断扩大
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达近日在官方博客中高调宣称，其美国制造合作伙伴和供应商网络已扩展至43个州，台积电位于凤凰城的工厂正在量产Blackwell晶圆，并计划在未来四年内与台积电、富士康、纬创、康宁、Coherent和Amkor等合作伙伴，在美国生产价值高达5000亿美元的AI基础设施。与此同时，英特尔在其“America 250”宣传稿中强调，自身在美国已具备从设计、制造到先进封装的全链条能力。

这两份宣传在晶圆制造环节都符合事实，但都刻意回避了同一个下游环节：每一片从台积电亚利桑那工厂出厂的Blackwell晶圆都必须横跨太平洋进行封装；目前没有任何高带宽存储器（HBM）在美国本土制造或封装；而计划填补这些空白的工厂，最早要到2028年才能投产。

### 宏大项目背后的现实

富士康正在休斯顿建设一座工厂，用于生产英伟达的GB300托盘模块；纬创将在德克萨斯州沃斯堡的新工厂组装和测试英伟达AI系统。Coherent于6月在德克萨斯州谢尔曼破土动工扩建工厂，该公司称这是首座6英寸磷化铟量产晶圆厂，将为连接AI系统提供激光器和光学组件。康宁则在北卡罗来纳州和德克萨斯州的光学制造基地新增超过3000个工作岗位。英伟达的博文还援引咨询公司Public First的估算，称由英伟达驱动的AI需求将在2026年为美国GDP贡献4850亿美元，并支持超过10万个就业岗位。英伟达CEO黄仁勋在文中宣称：“AI正推动一个千载难逢的机遇，重振美国制造业和供应链。”

与此同时，英特尔在博文中列出了在俄勒冈州、亚利桑那州、新墨西哥州和加利福尼亚州的研发和制造布局，将俄亥俄州描述为“规划中的场地”，并用大量篇幅介绍劳动力培训项目、K-12 AI教育以及与美国250周年纪念的合作。两份博文都没有提及最先进的AI处理器实际上是**在哪里组装成成品芯片的**。

### 跨越太平洋的往返旅程

英伟达和台积电去年10月在凤凰城附近的Fab 21工厂生产了首片Blackwell晶圆，此后该工厂已转而采用台积电专为英伟达定制的4纳米级工艺（4NP节点）量产Blackwell芯片。在凤凰城大都市区的另一边，英特尔的Fab 52工厂已于同月全面投产，成为英特尔18A工艺的首个量产基地。英特尔首席技术和运营官Naga Chandrasekaran去年12月对CNBC表示，该工厂每周可生产超过1万片18A晶圆。Panther Lake芯片已于1月实现广泛供货，Clearwater Forest定于今年上半年面世，18A工艺的良率预计在2027年初达到行业标准水平。

这意味着，先进逻辑晶圆现在确实由两家公司在美国本土、用两种竞争性工艺节点制造。与2020年代初相比，这无疑是一个巨大的成就，两家公司在自己的企业博客中都没有夸大这一点。

然而，一块Blackwell数据中心GPU的构成是：两片光罩尺寸的计算芯片与八堆HBM3e内存，通过硅中介层采用台积电的CoWoS-L封装技术整合在一起。而台积电所有的CoWoS产能都位于台湾。台积电在美国的工厂目前将100%的芯片（包括凤凰城生产的晶圆）送往台湾进行封装。因此，一块在亚利桑那制造的Blackwell晶圆需要飞行大约7000英里，才能被切割、堆叠和安装，然后继续运往系统组装地点，最后才能进入美国的数据中心。

至于HBM，目前生产中的每一堆都来自韩国SK海力士和三星的工厂，或者美光在中国台湾和日本的工厂；中介层下方的ABF基板同样高度集中在日本和中国台湾。美国目前没有任何工厂能够制造或封装HBM。目前唯一在美国本土大规模运行先进封装的是英特尔，其新墨西哥州的Foveros封装厂负责处理自家的3D堆叠产品，并已开始吸引外部客户——据报道，谷歌已预订英特尔2028年封装超过300万颗TPU的产能。然而，英特尔目前并不在英伟达公布的制造合作伙伴名单中。

### 最早2029年前无法实现

Amkor去年10月也在亚利桑那州皮奥里亚破土动工，建设一个价值70亿美元、分两期推进的项目，包括多达75万平方英尺的洁净室，获得约4亿美元的《芯片法案》资助，苹果和英伟达已签约成为主要客户。其第一座工厂将于2027年中期完工，2028年初开始生产。台积电于6月16日正式与Amkor签署了为期十年的协议，将向其采购封装和测试服务。而台积电高管在4月表示，台积电自己在亚利桑那的封装厂将在2029年之前上线CoWoS和3D IC产能。

SK海力士于4月在印第安纳州西拉斐特启动了38.7亿美元的先进封装工厂的初期工作，目标是在2028年下半年量产HBM4E和HBM5，这与Amkor的目标时间窗口一致。这意味着，整个Blackwell系列，甚至很可能第一代Rubin系列，都将在其完整的产品生命周期内无法实现完全本土化的制造路径。第一批能够在晶圆制造、封装和配备美国本土封装内存后无需离开美国本土的AI加速器，将是HBM4E时代的芯片，预计在2028年至2029年左右问世。

具有讽刺意味的是，去年7月提高至35%的Section 48D先进制造税收抵免，不适用于2026年12月31日之后开始建设的项目。这意味着Coherent的6月破土、SK海力士的4月打桩、以及Amkor的10月开工，如果想要享受这一税收优惠，都面临着共同的财务截止日期。

至于富士康和纬创分别在休斯顿和沃斯堡的工厂，它们将接收在台湾封装好的GPU，然后在美国本土将它们组装成托盘、机架和系统。正是这种组装工作，支撑了5000亿美元这个数字的绝大部分——该数字计算的是“生产的AI基础设施价值”，而非工厂的资本支出。晶圆是美国的，机架是美国的，但两者之间的一切都不是。这一局面能否按计划改变，取决于2028年，并高度依赖于亚利桑那州的两个封装园区和印第安纳州的一个封装园区能否按时完工。

history.title: China’s Huawei to enter South Korean AI chip market with new Atlas SuperPods, clusters pack 8,192 Ascend 950 accelerators per deployment — reportedly challenges Nvidia dominance with 'tripled inference performance' of H20 at one-quarter the cost
history.description: Huawei is reportedly preparing to enter South Korea's AI accelerator market with its Ascend 950 chips and Atlas 950 SuperPod, challenging Nvidia through aggressive pricing, amid a broader push to expand its AI ecosystem beyond China
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Huawei is planning to enter South Korea's AI accelerator market in the fourth quarter of 2026 with its Ascend 950 series processors and Atlas 950 SuperPod AI computing platform, according to a report by Korean publication ETNews last week. The move would mark Huawei's first major push into one of Nvidia's strongest overseas AI markets, with the Chinese company reportedly planning to undercut Nvidia on price while pitching its hardware as an alternative for customers seeking to reduce dependence on the US chipmaker.The chips spearheading the move are the Ascend 950PR and the Ascend 950DT, the latest models in Huawei's Ascend line of neural network processing units (NPUs) for AI computing. The Ascend 950PR, an inference-focused chip, entered mass production in April, while the Ascend 950DT, designed for AI training workloads, is scheduled for release in the fourth quarter. Both processors are expected to debut in Korea together with the Atlas 950 SuperPod, an integrated AI computing platform that Huawei says can scale to as many as 8,192 Ascend processors in a single deployment. (Image credit: Huawei)According to the report, Huawei Korea has completed master distributor agreements with two local partners, Hansol PNS and longtime Huawei collaborator SK Shieldus, and has already begun preparations for commercialization, including technical training, pricing policies, marketing strategies, and localized branding for the Korean market. Huawei is reportedly building its Korean campaign around aggressive pricing and processing power.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The company claims its Ascend 950PR delivers approximately 2.87 times the inference performance of Nvidia's H20 AI accelerator while costing around one-quarter as much. The H20 is Nvidia's export-compliant AI processor developed specifically for the Chinese market after US export restrictions prevented sales of more powerful GPUs. Huawei concedes its chip falls short on raw performance compared to Nvidia's flagship H200, but argues the gap can be closed by clustering thousands of Ascend processors together via the Atlas 950 platform.The Ascend 950 series uses Huawei's "self-developed" high-bandwidth memory (HBM), which the firm constructs from dies obtained from foreign sources. The 950PR uses Huawei's HiBL 1.0 memory and the 950DT its HiZQ 2.0 standard. You may like Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia’s H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo Huawei braces for $12 billion in AI chip revenue driven by homegrown AI model demand — Chinese fabs can barely keep up as Nvidia's market share craters within the region Huawei-led team claims it post-trained DeepSeek's 1.6-trillion-parameter model — 1,000 Ascend 910C chips used in training Huawei's strategy for the South Korea move, which comes as the country's demand for AI infrastructure surges, appears aimed at competing on both cost and ecosystem maturity, positioning its hardware as a viable Nvidia alternative. Nvidia's flagship accelerators reportedly command tens of thousands of dollars each, with supply remaining tight. A chip at one-quarter the price of the H20 gives Korean buyers a real incentive to seek a second source. Huawei also says it is improving compatibility between its Compute Architecture for Neural Networks (CANN) software stack and Nvidia's CUDA programming ecosystem to ease migration for developers.Huawei is not new to penetrating South Korea's market, having successfully entered the country’s highly competitive LTE equipment market in 2013. However, past experience is not a guarantee of future success. ETNews notes that industry observers expect Huawei to face resistance in Korea, citing local sensitivity toward Chinese technology, security concerns, the power and heat overhead of high-density Chinese silicon, and the vendor lock-in risk of adopting a proprietary stack. There is also a domestic dimension, as the Korean AI-chip scene is made up largely of accelerator startups, making Huawei's arrival — backed by its supply scale and software depth — read as a competitive threat.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: 中国华为进军韩国AI芯片市场，推出全新Atlas SuperPods，每套部署集成8192个昇腾950加速器——据称以四分之一成本实现H20三倍推理性能，挑战英伟达主导地位
history.descriptionZh:
华为计划于2026年第四季度进军韩国AI加速器市场，将携其Ascend 950系列处理器和Atlas 950 SuperPod AI计算平台亮相。据韩国媒体ETNews上周报道，此举标志着华为首次在英伟达最强势的海外AI市场之一发起重大冲击。华为计划以低于英伟达的价格策略，同时向寻求减少对美国芯片制造商依赖的客户推销其硬件，作为替代方案。

此次行动的核心是Ascend 950PR和Ascend 950DT——华为Ascend系列神经网络处理单元（NPU）的最新型号，专为AI计算设计。其中，Ascend 950PR是一款面向推理的芯片，已于今年4月进入量产；而Ascend 950DT则针对AI训练工作负载，计划于2026年第四季度发布。两款处理器预计将与Atlas 950 SuperPod一同在韩国首次亮相。Atlas 950 SuperPod是华为推出的集成AI计算平台，据称单次部署可扩展至多达8192颗Ascend处理器。

根据报道，华为韩国已完成与两家本地合作伙伴的主分销协议，分别是Hansol PNS和长期合作方SK Shieldus，并已开始商业化准备，包括技术培训、定价政策、营销策略以及针对韩国市场的本地化品牌推广。华为正围绕激进定价和算力优势构建其韩国攻势。

华为声称，其Ascend 950PR的推理性能约为英伟达H20 AI加速器的2.87倍，而成本仅为后者的四分之一左右。H20是英伟达专门为中国市场开发的符合出口规则的AI处理器，此前美国出口限制阻止了更强大GPU的销售。华为承认其芯片在原始性能上不及英伟达旗舰级H200，但认为通过Atlas 950平台将数千颗Ascend处理器集群化，可以缩小差距。

Ascend 950系列采用华为“自研”的高带宽内存（HBM），该内存由从海外来源获得的裸片构建而成。其中950PR使用华为HiBL 1.0内存，950DT则采用HiZQ 2.0标准。

华为进入韩国市场的时机正值该国对AI基础设施需求激增之际。其策略似乎旨在同时在成本和生态成熟度上展开竞争，将其硬件定位为英伟达的可行替代方案。英伟达旗舰级加速器据称单价高达数万美元，且供应依然紧张。一颗价格仅为H20四分之一的芯片，为韩国买家提供了寻求第二供应商的真实动力。华为还表示，正在改善其计算架构神经网络（CANN）软件栈与英伟达CUDA编程生态系统的兼容性，以降低开发者的迁移难度。

华为对进入韩国市场并不陌生，曾于2013年成功打入该国竞争激烈的LTE设备市场。然而，过往经验并不能保证未来成功。ETNews指出，行业观察人士预计华为将在韩国面临阻力，理由包括当地对中国技术的敏感性、安全问题、高密度中国硅芯片带来的功耗和散热挑战，以及采用专有堆栈所导致的供应商锁定风险。此外，还有本土维度：韩国AI芯片领域主要由加速器初创公司构成，华为带着其供应规模和软件深度到来，被视为一种竞争威胁。

history.title: Wisconsin residents file class-action lawsuit against Microsoft's 'world's most powerful AI data center' due to data center noise — plaintiffs also mention construction noise and extreme light pollution from $7.3 billion facility
history.description: Wisconsin residents file class-action lawsuit against Microsoft due to data center noise — plaintiffs also mention construction noise and extreme light pollution
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Controversy due to AI data center buildouts generally centers around their massive power usage and draining of local water reserves, but noise is a third torment to nearby residents, and one that is arguably much harder to correct. Residents of Sturtevant, slightly south of Milwaukee, Wisconsin, filed a class-action lawsuit against Microsoft due to the excessive noise produced by the company's Fairwater facility. CEO Satya Nadella described the project as "the world's most powerful AI data center," projected to generate 865,000 tokens per second and have a final bill of $7.3 billion.The Sturtevant residents live just 1.5 miles (2.4 km) from the facility. The lawsuit was filed by three citizens and represents the households within this distance, reportedly amounting to over 1,000 homes, including areas in Mount Pleasant. The filing describes the noise situation as "not only excessive, but consistent and pervasive," and claims Microsoft did not "implement adequate acoustic barriers, shields, or walls that absorb, mitigate, and/or prevent the escape of noise, thereby resulting in the offsite emission of excessive noise beyond its property." One resident claims he had to change his shift at work to be able to sleep at all.Nearby residents also complain about excessive dust and traffic stemming from the construction work, as well as light pollution. One resident says he often can't see his house coming into town, while another claims that before the data center arrived, the sky was dark and full of stars, now mostly gone due to the bright lights.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: To some credit, Microsoft appears to have been trying to improve the situation, judging by a fairly detailed blog post on its community website. On June 18, the company said its engineers applied several measures that "fully resolved the issue," and that it would apply additional mitigations over the following months, including "additional sound reduction components."The backstory in the post says the firm was aware of the issue back in April, and "[it] did not expect the tonal quality of the sound to travel as far as it has," attributing the decibels to cooling fans operating at too-high speeds, now purportedly corrected. In an earlier project update about the data center, Microsoft says it would have street sweepers working 10 hours a day and limit construction to hours between 6am and 10pm. You may like AI data centers face increasing complaints about inaudible but 'felt' infrasound Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center The lawsuit was filed on July 1, indicating that either the issue isn't resolved, or that the particularly short distance between Fairwater 1 and Sturtevant might make for a conundrum that's exceedingly tricky to solve. Some Mount Pleasant residents even live across the street from the campus, too. Before the Fairwater data center arrived, the land was already zoned for heavy industrial use back in 2017 to Foxconn, a status that carried over to Microsoft upon purchase. Notably, Wisconsin's "direct legislation" apparently does not allow amending or repealing existing ordinances, and is only available to cities, not towns. history.titleZh: 威斯康星州居民因噪音问题对微软"世界最强大AI数据中心"提起集体诉讼——原告指责73亿美元设施造成建筑噪音和极端光污染
history.descriptionZh:
围绕AI数据中心建设引发的争议通常集中在巨大的电力消耗和当地水资源枯竭上，但噪音问题已成为困扰附近居民的第三大痛点，而且可以说是更难以纠正的难题。位于威斯康星州密尔沃基市以南的斯特蒂文特（Sturtevant）居民，因微软Fairwater数据中心产生的过度噪音，对其提起了集体诉讼。微软CEO萨提亚·纳德拉将该设施描述为“世界上最强大的AI数据中心”，预计每秒可生成86.5万个token（AI处理的基本单位），最终投资额高达73亿美元。

提起指控的斯特蒂文特居民距离该设施仅1.5英里（约2.4公里）。该诉讼由三名公民发起，代表这一距离范围内的家庭，据称涉及超过1000户住宅，包括快乐山（Mount Pleasant）的部分区域。诉状描述噪音状况“不仅过度，而且持续不断、无处不在”，并声称微软未能“实施足够的隔音屏障、防护墙或能够吸收、减轻和/或阻止噪音泄漏的墙体，从而导致过度噪音从其地产范围外泄”。一位居民声称，为了能睡个好觉，他不得不调整工作班次。

附近的居民还抱怨施工带来的过量粉尘和交通问题，以及光污染。一位居民说他经常无法在入城时看到自己的房子，而另一名居民则声称，在数据中心到来之前，天空是黑暗的，满天繁星，现在这一切大多因明亮的灯光而消失了。

值得肯定的是，从微软社区网站上相当详细的博客文章来看，该公司似乎一直在努力改善这一状况。6月18日，该公司表示其工程师采取了多项措施，“完全解决了问题”，并将在未来几个月内采取更多缓解措施，包括“额外的降噪组件”。

这篇后续文章的背景信息显示，该公司早在4月份就意识到了这个问题，并“[它]没有预料到声音的音调质量会传播得如此之远”，将分贝水平归因于以过高速度运行的冷却风扇，现已声称纠正。在一份关于该数据中心的早期项目更新报告中，微软表示每天将有街道清扫车工作10小时，并将施工时间限制在早上6点到晚上10点之间。

诉讼是在7月1日提起的，这表明要么问题尚未解决，要么Fairwater 1设施与斯特蒂文特之间特别短的距离可能构成了一个极其棘手的难题。有些快乐山的居民甚至就住在园区街对面。在Fairwater数据中心入驻之前，该地块早在2017年就已规划为面向富士康的重工业用途，这一规划状态在微软购买后得以延续。值得注意的是，威斯康星州的“直接立法”似乎不允许修改或废除现有的条例，并且仅对城市适用，而非城镇。

history.title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
history.description:
Most Americans don't trust AI. It's proven that it doesn't know what safe toppings for pizza are. People don't even want to listen to AI music. But none of that matters for some of America's wealthy, who are turning to AI to teach their kids instead of traditional schools.

Unsurprisingly, Silicon Valley have been major adopters of this new model. Shaun Johnson, a San Francisco-based venture capitalist, told The Wall Street Journal that he plans to send his …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Most Americans don’t trust AI. It’s proven that it doesn’t know what safe toppings for pizza are. People don’t even want to listen to AI music. But none of that matters for some of America’s wealthy, who are turning to AI to teach their kids instead of traditional schools.Companies like Forge Prep and Alpha School are charging families tens of thousands of dollars to turn their kids into beta testers for AI tutors and “interactive project-based workshops.” Unsurprisingly, Silicon Valley have been major adopters of this new model. Shaun Johnson, a San Francisco-based venture capitalist, told The Wall Street Journal that he plans to send his son to a $75,000 year Alpha Kindergarten. He said, “We recognize that education is likely broken the way it is and there’s going to be entrepreneurs that try to fix it… You want someone to be able to think on their feet and navigate the world, not necessarily a recitation of facts in a particular discipline.”Ignoring Johnson’s fundamental lack of understanding about modern pedagogy, it’s unclear how notoriously sycophantic AI will train children to “think on their feet and navigate the world.” It’s also concerning that Alpha School cofounder MacKenzie Price has said she plans to keep “hot-button social issues” out of the classroom. Which, in the current political climate, could cover women’s rights, America’s history of slavery, and our immigrant past. That might not seem like a major issue when you’re talking about kindergarten, but in some locations, Alpha School goes through high school.Companies like Forge also don’t share performance metrics, so there’s no evidence that these AI-guided private schools are improving educational outcomes.
history.author: Terrence O’Brien
history.titleZh: 该国一些富人让AI教他们的孩子
history.descriptionZh:
大多数美国人对人工智能持不信任态度——有证据表明，AI连披萨上什么配料安全都不知道，人们甚至不愿听AI生成的音乐。但这些都不妨碍一部分美国富裕阶层将子女从传统学校转向AI教育。以Forge Prep和Alpha School为代表的公司向家庭收取数万美元费用，让孩子们成为AI导师及“互动式项目工作坊”的测试者。不出意料，硅谷成为这一新模式的主要拥趸。旧金山风险投资人肖恩·约翰逊（Shaun Johnson）向《华尔街日报》表示，他计划将儿子送入一年学费7.5万美元的Alpha幼儿园。他说：“我们认识到传统教育方式很可能已经失效，会有创业者试图修正它……我们希望孩子能灵活思考、应对世界，而不是死记硬背某个学科的事实。”

暂且不论约翰逊对现代教育学的根本性误解，关键在于——以谄媚迎合著称的AI如何训练孩子“灵活思考、应对世界”？更令人担忧的是，Alpha School联合创始人麦肯齐·普莱斯（MacKenzie Price）声称，她计划在课堂中避开“热点社会议题”。在当下的政治气候中，这可能会涵盖女性权利、美国奴隶制历史以及移民传统。对于幼儿园来说，这或许不是大问题，但在某些地区，Alpha School一直开设到高中。

此外，像Forge这样的公司并不公开绩效指标，因此没有任何证据表明这些AI引导的私立学校确实改善了教育成果。
